senses.gloss: to take

examples.text: وَلَمَّا سَكَتَ عَنْ مُوسَى الْغَضَبُ أَخَذَ الأَلْوَاحَ[…]
examples.english: When Moses’ anger subsided, he took up the tablets...

senses.gloss: to take along
to get, to receive, to obtain
to seize, to grab, to take hold

examples.english: And he (Moses) threw down the Tablets and seized his brother (Aaron) by [the hair of] his head, pulling him toward him.

senses.gloss: to capture, to seize, to take captive

examples.text: فَإِذَا ٱنْسَلَخَ الْأَشْهُرُ الْحُرُمُ فَاقْتُلُوا الْمُشْرِكِينَ حَيْثُ وَجَدتُّمُوهُمْ وَخُذُوهُمْ وَاحْصُرُوهُمْ وَاقْعُدُوا لَهُمْ كُلَّ مَرْصَدٍ[…]
examples.english: But once the Sacred Months have passed, kill the polytheists ˹who violated their treaties and fought˺ wherever you find them, capture them, besiege them, and lie in wait for them on every way.

senses.gloss: to perceive, to notice
to gather, to understand, to infer, to deduce
to grip, to captivate, to spellbind
to keep, to observe, to adopt, to embrace, to follow
to accept

examples.text: […]قَالَ أَأَقْرَرْتُمْ وَأَخَذْتُمْ عَلَىٰ ذَٰلِكُمْ إِصْرِي قَالُوا أَقْرَرْنَا[…]
examples.english: [God] said, “Do you affirm and accept upon that My Burden (i.e. Allah’s Covenant)?” They [the prophets] said, “We affirm.”

senses.gloss: to admonish, to urge, to drive
to enjoin, to impose
to strip, to deprive, to cut off, to bar
to reproach, to blame, to criticize

examples.english: The author was reproached for his neglect of the sources.

senses.gloss: to obligate
to learn, to study
to begin, to start, to prepare to, to set out to, to be about to, to get ready to (followed by a non-past verb)
verbal noun of أَخَذَ (ʔaḵaḏa) (form I)
acceptance, reception
seizure
taking away, removal
to put under a spell, to enchant, to bewitch

etymology.text: From Proto-Semitic *ʔaḫaḏ-. Cognate with Hebrew אָחַז (aḥáz), Aramaic ܐܚܕ (ʾeḥaḏ), Sabaean 𐩱𐩭𐩹 (ʾḵḏ).
